title: Juggling Talk
titel_tagline: slides powered by reveal.js & claude code
siteswaps_li1_sub2: In each time step, 0 or 1 ball is thrown
siteswaps_li2: Juggling patterns are described as a sequence of numbers
siteswaps_li3: The number describes how many time steps later the ball lands again
siteswaps_li4_sub1: Odd number: throw crosses to the other hand
siteswaps_li4_sub2: Even number: throw lands in the same hand
valid_li1:
At each time step, exactly 1 ball must land
(except for a "0" throw, then no ball may land here)
valid_li2:
One possible definition:
a siteswap $a_0a_1a_2\dots a_{n-1}$ is valid,
if the set $S=\{(a_i+i)\bmod n | 0\leq i\leq n-1\}$
has exactly $n$ distinct elements.
valid_li3: Example: 123 works ($S=\{{\color{green}1},{\color{red}0},{\color{blue}2}\}$), 321 doesn't ($S=\{0\}$)
valid_li4: The average of the numbers $a_i$ equals the number of balls
state_h2: Can these patterns be chained together?
state_body:
That depends on the state!

Patterns like 3 are in the ground state

Patterns like 51 (Shower) are in an excited state
transitions_heading: All transitions with maximum throw height 5 and 3 balls:
transitions_li1: we get from 3 to 51 with e.g. 4 or 52
transitions_li2: we get back with e.g. 2 or 41
multiplex_li1:
The number of hands is arbitrary
→ we just need to define a throwing order!
multiplex_li2: Popular variant: 4 hands, distributed across 2 people
multiplex_li3: Both throw alternately, each alternating both hands
multiplex_li4: Results in e.g.: a 6 is a self (changes hand, but not person), 7 is a pass (changes person)
multiplex_li5: one person throws the 7 straight, the other across
more_h3: There is of course much more ...
more_li1:
What if we also throw simultaneously with both/multiple hands?
→ Notation with parentheses and x for crossing throws, e.g. everyone knows (6x,4)(4,6x), short 6x4
more_li2:
What if we throw and catch multiple balls at once?
→ Notation with square brackets
more_li3: What if the hands also move around somehow?
more_li4: What if the numbers are negative?!?
more_li4_sub1: Here we learn from Dirac's physics:
more_li4_sub4: Ball/anti-ball pairs are created and annihilate during the juggling pattern
